data.content:
### Deploying Supabase Edge Function (Bash) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/yjs-react-supabase-text-collab/README.md This command deploys the 'merge-document-updates' edge function to the configured Supabase project using the Supabase CLI. This function is an optional component for merging Yjs document updates. ```Bash supabase functions deploy merge-document-updates ``` -------------------------------- ### Invoking Supabase Edge Function via Curl (Bash) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/yjs-react-supabase-text-collab/README.md This curl command demonstrates how to trigger the deployed 'merge-document-updates' Supabase edge function via an HTTP POST request. It requires the project reference, anonymous key, and the document ID as parameters. ```Bash curl -L -X POST 'https://.supabase.co/functions/v1/merge-document-updates' -H 'Authorization: Bearer [anon-key]' --data '{"document_id":"[document-id]"}' ``` -------------------------------- ### Copying Environment File (Bash) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/react-supabase-todolist-optional-sync/README.md Creates a local copy of the environment variable template file. This copy (`.env.local`) is where you will insert your Supabase and PowerSync credentials. ```bash cp .env .env.local ``` -------------------------------- ### Build Production Codebase (pnpm) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/react-supabase-todolist/README.md This command builds the production-ready version of the demo application. This is necessary for deploying the application or enabling PWA features. ```bash pnpm build ``` -------------------------------- ### Installing Project Dependencies (Shell) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/angular-supabase-todolist/README.md Installs all necessary project dependencies as listed in the package.json file. This command requires that pnpm is installed on your system. ```Shell pnpm install ``` -------------------------------- ### Installing Dependencies and Building Packages (Bash) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/vue-supabase-todolist/README.md Installs project dependencies using pnpm and then builds the local packages required for the demo. This is the initial step after cloning the repository. ```bash pnpm install pnpm build:packages ``` -------------------------------- ### Run Development Server (pnpm) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/react-supabase-todolist/README.md This command starts the development server for the demo application using pnpm. The application will typically be accessible locally for testing and development. ```bash pnpm dev ``` -------------------------------- ### Creating Environment File (Bash) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/vue-supabase-todolist/README.md Copies the environment variable template file (.env.local.template) to the actual environment file (.env.local). The user must then edit .env.local with their specific Supabase and PowerSync credentials. ```bash cp .env.local.template .env.local ``` -------------------------------- ### Install Dependencies and Build Packages (pnpm) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/react-supabase-todolist/README.md This command block installs the necessary project dependencies using pnpm and then builds the packages within the repository. This is the first step after cloning the repository. ```bash pnpm install pnpm build:packages ``` -------------------------------- ### Copying Environment Variable Template (Bash) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/react-native-web-supabase-todolist/README.md Copies the environment variable template file (`.env.local.template`) to `.env.local`. This is required to configure the application with specific Supabase and PowerSync credentials. ```bash cp .env.local.template .env.local ``` -------------------------------- ### Installing Project Dependencies using pnpm - Bash Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/react-multi-client/README.md This command installs the required project dependencies using pnpm and then builds the packages. This is the first step after cloning the repository. ```bash pnpm install pnpm build:packages ``` -------------------------------- ### Creating Environment File (Shell) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/angular-supabase-todolist/README.md Copies the environment template file (.env.template) to create the actual .env file. This file is used for storing configuration details like PowerSync and Supabase credentials and must be populated manually after copying. ```Shell cp .env.template .env ``` -------------------------------- ### Installing Project Dependencies (Bash) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/react-supabase-todolist-optional-sync/README.md Installs project dependencies using pnpm and builds necessary packages for the PowerSync monorepo setup. This step is required before running the demo application. ```bash pnpm install pnpm build:packages ``` -------------------------------- ### Preview Production Build (pnpm) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/react-supabase-todolist/README.md This command starts a local server to preview the production build of the application. This allows testing the optimized production code and PWA features before deployment. ```bash pnpm preview ``` -------------------------------- ### Building Production Code (Bash) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/vue-supabase-todolist/README.md Builds the application code for production deployment. This command generates optimized static assets and includes the PWA manifest and service worker. ```bash pnpm build ``` -------------------------------- ### Building Angular Project (Shell) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/angular-supabase-todolist/README.md Compiles the Angular application into output files for deployment. The build artifacts are placed in the `dist/` directory by default. ```Shell ng build ``` -------------------------------- ### Installing Dependencies for React Native Web Demo (Bash) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/react-native-web-supabase-todolist/README.md Use pnpm to install project dependencies and build packages in the repository root. This prepares the project for running. ```bash pnpm install pnpm build:packages ``` -------------------------------- ### Starting Application Server (Shell) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/angular-supabase-todolist/README.md Starts the local development server to host the demo application. This command should be run after or in conjunction with 'pnpm watch' to serve the compiled application. ```Shell pnpm start ``` -------------------------------- ### Generating Angular Component (Shell) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/angular-supabase-todolist/README.md Uses the Angular CLI to generate a new component boilerplate. Replace `component-name` with the desired name for your new component. ```Shell ng generate component component-name ``` -------------------------------- ### Deploying PowerSync Sync Rules (YAML) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/react-supabase-todolist-optional-sync/README.md Defines the data synchronization rules for PowerSync using YAML. It specifies how user-specific data (lists and todos) is organized into buckets based on the user ID for synchronization. ```yaml bucket_definitions: user_lists: # Separate bucket per todo list parameters: select id as list_id from lists where owner_id = request.user_id() data: - select * from lists where id = bucket.list_id - select * from todos where list_id = bucket.list_id ``` -------------------------------- ### Running Development Server (Bash) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/react-supabase-todolist-optional-sync/README.md Starts the local development server for the React demo application using pnpm. This allows you to view and interact with the application in a development environment. ```bash pnpm dev ``` -------------------------------- ### Running Development Server (Bash) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/vue-supabase-todolist/README.md Starts the development server for the demo application. This allows running the app locally, typically with hot-reloading, for testing and development purposes. The PWA features are not available in this mode. ```bash pnpm dev ``` -------------------------------- ### Change Directory to Demo Folder (bash) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/react-supabase-todolist/README.md This command changes the current working directory to the specific demo application folder within the repository, allowing subsequent commands to be run in the correct context. ```bash cd demos/react-supabase-todolist ``` -------------------------------- ### Running the Development Server using pnpm - Bash Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/react-multi-client/README.md This command starts the local development server for the demo application. After execution, the application should be accessible in a web browser at the specified local address. ```bash pnpm dev ``` -------------------------------- ### Running Production Server (Bash) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/react-supabase-todolist-optional-sync/README.md Starts a local server to serve the production build of the application using pnpm. Use this to test the built version and PWA features locally. ```bash pnpm preview ``` -------------------------------- ### Navigating to Demo Directory (Bash) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/vue-supabase-todolist/README.md Changes the current directory to the specific demo application folder (vue-supabase-todolist). This step is necessary to execute commands related to the demo project itself. ```bash cd demos/vue-supabase-todolist ``` -------------------------------- ### Defining PowerSync Sync Rules for User Data (YAML) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/react-native-web-supabase-todolist/README.md Defines PowerSync sync rules using buckets to synchronize data per user. It specifies that lists and todos are synced based on the user's ID (`request.user_id()`) within individual buckets. ```yaml bucket_definitions: user_lists: # Separate bucket per todo list parameters: select id as list_id from lists where owner_id = request.user_id() data: - select * from lists where id = bucket.list_id - select * from todos where list_id = bucket.list_id ``` -------------------------------- ### Navigating to Demo Directory (Bash) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/react-supabase-todolist-optional-sync/README.md Changes the current directory to the specific demo application directory within the repository structure. This must be done before executing demo-specific commands. ```bash cd demos/react-supabase-todolist-optional-sync ``` -------------------------------- ### Getting Angular CLI Help (Shell) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/angular-supabase-todolist/README.md Displays help information for the Angular CLI commands. Use `ng help ` to get detailed help on a specific command. ```Shell ng help ``` -------------------------------- ### Fetching Data with useSuspenseQuery and Suspense - JSX Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/packages/tanstack-react-query/README.md Shows how to integrate PowerSync queries with React's Suspense feature using the `useSuspenseQuery` hook. It defines a component that uses the hook and wraps it within a `Suspense` boundary, demonstrating how to provide a fallback while the query is loading. ```JSX // TodoListDisplaySuspense.jsx import { Suspense } from 'react'; import { useSuspenseQuery } from '@powersync/tanstack-react-query'; const TodoListContent = () => { const { data: todoLists } = useSuspenseQuery({ queryKey: ['todoLists'], query: 'SELECT * FROM lists', }); return (
    {todoLists.map((list) => (
  • {list.name}
    • ))}
); }; export const TodoListDisplaySuspense = () => { return ( /* Will show fallback while query is loading */ Loading todo lists...}> ); }; ``` -------------------------------- ### Fetching Data with useQuery and Kysely - TSX Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/packages/tanstack-react-query/README.md Demonstrates the flexibility of the `useQuery` hook by accepting a Kysely query builder object instead of a string. This allows leveraging Kysely's type inference for the returned data, providing enhanced type safety when using the integration. ```TSX // TodoListDisplay.tsx import { useQuery } from '@powersync/tanstack-react-query'; export const TodoListDisplay = () => { const { data: todoLists, isLoading, error } = useQuery({ queryKey: ['todoLists'], query: kyselyDb.selectFrom('lists').selectAll(), // The type of the rows in `data` are inferred from the Kysely query }); if (isLoading) { return
Loading todo lists...
; } if (error) { return
Error loading todo lists: {error.message}
; } return (
    {todoLists?.map((list) => (
  • {list.name}
    • ))}
); }; ``` -------------------------------- ### Watching and Building Application (Shell) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/angular-supabase-todolist/README.md Starts the build process in watch mode. It compiles the application and automatically recompiles whenever source files are changed, preparing the application for development serving. ```Shell pnpm watch ``` -------------------------------- ### Building Production Code (Bash) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/react-supabase-todolist-optional-sync/README.md Builds the production-ready version of the demo application using pnpm. This step is necessary to test features like the Progressive Web App (PWA) capabilities outside of development mode. ```bash pnpm build ``` -------------------------------- ### Copy Environment Template File (bash) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/react-supabase-todolist/README.md This command copies the template file for environment variables (`.env.local.template`) to the actual file (`.env.local`) that will be used by the application. The user must then edit the copied file with their specific credentials. ```bash cp .env.local.template .env.local ``` -------------------------------- ### Running React Native Android App (Bash) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/react-native-web-supabase-todolist/README.md Executes the pnpm command to start the React Native application on an Android emulator or connected device. This command builds and launches the native Android app. ```bash pnpm android ``` -------------------------------- ### Fetching Data with useQuery and SQL String - JSX Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/packages/tanstack-react-query/README.md Illustrates how to fetch data from PowerSync using the `useQuery` hook provided by the integration package. It uses a raw SQL query string with parameters, shows how to handle loading and error states, and renders a list of items based on the fetched data. ```JSX // TodoListDisplay.jsx import { useQuery } from '@powersync/tanstack-react-query'; export const TodoListDisplay = () => { const { data: todoLists, isLoading, isFetching, error } = useQuery({ queryKey: ['todoLists'], query: 'SELECT * FROM lists WHERE id = ?', // use `query` instead of `queryFn` to define a SQL query - this allows watching underlying tables for changes parameters: ['id-1'], // supply query parameters for the SQL query }); if (isLoading) { return
Loading todo lists...
; } if (error) { return
Error loading todo lists: {error.message}
; } return (
    {todoLists?.map((list) => (
  • {list.name}
    • ))}
); }; ``` -------------------------------- ### Running React Native Web App (Bash) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/react-native-web-supabase-todolist/README.md Executes the pnpm command to start the React Native Web application. This command bundles the code and serves it, making the application accessible in a web browser. ```bash pnpm web ``` -------------------------------- ### Copying Local Environment Template File - Bash Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/react-multi-client/README.md This command copies the example environment variable template file (`.env.local.template`) to the actual local environment file (`.env.local`). The copied file then needs to be edited with specific credentials. ```bash cp .env.local.template .env.local ``` -------------------------------- ### Running React Native iOS App (Bash) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/react-native-web-supabase-todolist/README.md Executes the pnpm command to start the React Native application on an iOS simulator or connected device. This command builds and launches the native iOS app. ```bash pnpm ios ``` -------------------------------- ### Initializing PowerSync and Drizzle Integration - JavaScript Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/packages/drizzle-driver/README.md This snippet shows how to define Drizzle tables and relations (`lists`, `todos`), create a `PowerSyncDatabase` instance with a schema (using `AppSchema`), and then use `wrapPowerSyncWithDrizzle` to combine them, providing a Drizzle-compatible database interface (`db`) backed by PowerSync. It requires the `@powersync/drizzle-driver`, `@powersync/web`, and `drizzle-orm` packages. ```javascript import { wrapPowerSyncWithDrizzle } from '@powersync/drizzle-driver'; import { PowerSyncDatabase } from '@powersync/web'; import { relations } from 'drizzle-orm'; import { index, integer, sqliteTable, text } from 'drizzle-orm/sqlite-core'; import { AppSchema } from './schema'; export const lists = sqliteTable('lists', { id: text('id'), name: text('name') }); export const todos = sqliteTable('todos', { id: text('id'), description: text('description'), list_id: text('list_id'), created_at: text('created_at') }); export const listsRelations = relations(lists, ({ one, many }) => ({ todos: many(todos) })); export const todosRelations = relations(todos, ({ one, many }) => ({ list: one(lists, { fields: [todos.list_id], references: [lists.id] }) })); export const drizzleSchema = { lists, todos, listsRelations, todosRelations }; // As an alternative to manually defining a PowerSync schema, generate the local PowerSync schema from the Drizzle schema with the `DrizzleAppSchema` constructor: // import { DrizzleAppSchema } from '@powersync/drizzle-driver'; // export const AppSchema = new DrizzleAppSchema(drizzleSchema); // // This is optional, but recommended, since you will only need to maintain one schema on the client-side // Read on to learn more. export const powerSyncDb = new PowerSyncDatabase({ database: { dbFilename: 'test.sqlite' }, schema: AppSchema }); // This is the DB you will use in queries export const db = wrapPowerSyncWithDrizzle(powerSyncDb, { schema: drizzleSchema }); ``` -------------------------------- ### Copying PowerSync Web Assets (Bash) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/react-native-web-supabase-todolist/README.md Executes a pnpm command provided by the PowerSync web package to copy necessary assets. This step is required for the React Native Web implementation to function correctly, particularly for web workers. ```bash pnpm powersync-web copy-assets ``` -------------------------------- ### Installing PowerSync OP-SQLite Package (Bash) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/packages/powersync-op-sqlite/README.md Installs the `@powersync/op-sqlite` package using `npx expo install`. This package provides the necessary factory to integrate OP-SQLite with the PowerSync React Native SDK. ```bash npx expo install @powersync/op-sqlite ``` -------------------------------- ### Copying PowerSync Worker Assets (Shell) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/angular-supabase-todolist/README.md Copies required PowerSync service worker assets from the installed powersync-web package to the specified output directory (src/assets). This step is necessary for the service worker to be accessible and correctly configured when the application runs. ```Shell pnpm powersync-web copy-assets -o src/assets ``` -------------------------------- ### Setting up PowerSync and TanStack Query Context - JSX Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/packages/tanstack-react-query/README.md Demonstrates the necessary setup to use `@powersync/tanstack-react-query` by providing both `PowerSyncContext` and `QueryClientProvider` in the application's root component. It shows how to initialize the PowerSync database and the TanStack Query client using `useMemo`. ```JSX // App.jsx import { PowerSyncDatabase } from '@powersync/web'; // or for React Native // import { PowerSyncDatabase } from '@powersync/react-native'; import { PowerSyncContext } from "@powersync/react"; import { QueryClient, QueryClientProvider } from '@tanstack/react-query'; export const App = () => { const powerSync = React.useMemo(() => { // Set up PowerSync client }, []) const queryClient = React.useMemo(() => new QueryClient(), []) return ( {/** Your components go here */} ); }; ``` -------------------------------- ### Running Angular Development Server (Shell) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/angular-supabase-todolist/README.md Starts the standard Angular development server, typically serving the application at `http://localhost:4200`. Note that this specific command (`ng serve`) may not support service worker applications like the one required for full PowerSync functionality. ```Shell ng serve ``` -------------------------------- ### Copying Environment Template File (Bash) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/yjs-react-supabase-text-collab/README.md This command copies the provided template file for environment variables (`.env.local.template`) to create the actual local environment configuration file (`.env.local`). Users must then edit `.env.local` with their specific Supabase and PowerSync credentials. ```Bash cp .env.local.template .env.local ``` -------------------------------- ### Generating Angular Schematic (Shell) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/demos/angular-supabase-todolist/README.md Uses the Angular CLI's `ng generate` command to create various types of code artifacts (schematics) like directives, pipes, services, etc. Choose the desired type and provide a name. ```Shell ng generate directive|pipe|service|class|guard|interface|enum|module ``` -------------------------------- ### Initializing PowerSync Vue Plugin (JavaScript) Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/packages/vue/README.md Demonstrates how to use the `createPowerSyncPlugin` function to set up and integrate a PowerSync client instance with a Vue application instance. This makes the PowerSync client available application-wide via dependency injection. ```javascript // main.js import { createApp } from 'vue' import App from './App.vue' import { createPowerSyncPlugin } from '@powersync/vue' const db = // Setup PowerSync client const app = createApp(App); const powerSyncPlugin = createPowerSyncPlugin({database: db}); app.use(powerSyncPlugin); app.mount('#app'); ``` -------------------------------- ### Typing Data with useQuery and useSuspenseQuery - TSX Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/packages/tanstack-react-query/README.md Illustrates how to apply TypeScript types to the data returned by the `useQuery` and `useSuspenseQuery` hooks. By specifying the type argument `<{ id: string, name: string }>`, developers can ensure type safety for the query results. ```TSX // TodoListDisplay.tsx const TodoListContent = () => { const { data } = useQuery<{ id: string, name: string }>({ queryKey: ['todoLists'], query: 'SELECT * FROM lists', }); const { data: todoListsSuspense } = useSuspenseQuery<{ id: string, name: string }>({ queryKey: ['todoListsSuspense'], query: 'SELECT * FROM lists', }); return <> } ``` -------------------------------- ### Making Drizzle Queries Compilable for Hooks - JavaScript Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/packages/drizzle-driver/README.md This snippet demonstrates the use of `toCompilableQuery` to transform a standard Drizzle query object (`query = db.select().from(lists)`) into a format that can be directly used within PowerSync data-fetching hooks or composables, such as `useQuery`, enabling seamless integration of Drizzle queries with PowerSync's reactive data layer. It requires the `@powersync/drizzle-driver` package. ```javascript import { toCompilableQuery } from '@powersync/drizzle-driver'; const query = db.select().from(lists); const { data: listRecords, isLoading } = useQuery(toCompilableQuery(query)); ``` -------------------------------- ### Installing PowerSync Web SDK - Bash Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/packages/web/README.md This command installs the main PowerSync SDK package specifically for web clients using npm. This package provides the core functionality for integrating PowerSync into a web application. ```bash npm install @powersync/web ``` -------------------------------- ### Using useSuspenseQuery with React Suspense and ErrorBoundary Source: https://github.com/powersync-ja/powersync-js/blob/main/packages/react/README.md Demonstrates integrating `useSuspenseQuery` with React's built-in Suspense and ErrorBoundary components. The hook suspends while data is loading, causing the `` to render. Errors during data fetching are caught by the ``. The actual data fetching component is wrapped inside Suspense and ErrorBoundary. Requires `@powersync/react`, `react`, and `react-error-boundary`. ```JSX // TodoListDisplaySuspense.jsx import { ErrorBoundary } from 'react-error-boundary'; import { Suspense } from 'react'; import { useSuspenseQuery } from '@powersync/react'; const TodoListContent = () => { const { data: todoLists } = useSuspenseQuery("SELECT * FROM lists"); return (
    {todoLists.map((list) => (
  • {list.name}
    • ))}
); }; export const TodoListDisplaySuspense = () => { return ( Something went wrong}> Loading todo lists...}> ); }; ```
